name: Velun
description: Tidally locked ice world. A mining colony bored deep into the warm side fault lines — the tunnels are still pressurised, but something has been moving the equipment.

name: Mireth
description: Cracked desert plains baking under Quases' intensifying radiation. A military garrison was the last to evacuate — their weapons cache was never opened.

name: Quell
description: A warm shallow ocean world. Research platforms float abandoned on the surface, slowly listing. The tide schedules were never updated — some airlocks flood twice a day.

name: Harven
description: Methane-purple skies, breathable if you filter. The largest colonist settlement in the system — 40,000 people left in six hours. The lights are still on.

name: Orix VII
description: Seventh of seven identical rocky bodies — scientists never understood why. The research station here was studying Quases' expansion. Their final data logs are intact but encrypted.

name: Calwen
description: The most habitable world in the system. The colonists here were last to receive the evacuation order — they had time to bury their supplies. Marked caches are scattered across the plains, if the markers haven't shifted.

name: Thrake
description: Volcanic, sulphurous, barely survivable even before the star began swelling. A military black site was built here precisely because no one would come looking. No evacuation record exists for it.

name: Crael
description: Ringed world — the rings are glass shards from a shattered moon. Ore refineries orbited the rings; most have decayed into them. One is still broadcasting a maintenance ping on a loop.

name: Akhemvar
description: 98° axial tilt. Perpetual twilight at the equator, violent seasonal extremes elsewhere. The colonist domes were built for the stable belt — most are still sealed, frozen solid, and full of abandoned provisions.

name: Presso
description: High-pressure, needle rain, corrosive atmosphere. Only heavily armoured mining rigs could operate here. The rigs are still running on autonomous cycles — no one told them to stop.
